elements.sent-2411-007: Persistent homology analysis has been invented and well-developed in recent decades, regarded as one of the main tools in topological data analysis (TDA for short) \cite{MR1949898 .}
elements.sent-2411-008: The standard workflow in the persistent homology analysis is that we first construct a filtration of complexes (or more generally, topological spaces) from the input data, and then record the homological cycles (holes) appearing in the filtration, not only the number but also the time of cycles when they get born and vanish.
elements.sent-2411-009: In more detail, a filtration of topological spaces can be defined as a covariant functor $\mathcal{F}\colon \bfP\to \mathrm{Top}$ from a poset $\bfP$ as a category to the category of topological spaces $\mathrm{Top}$ (morphisms are continuous maps).
